user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to create, update, and delete collection and item records, verify metadata, add catalogers to collections and items, monitor collection progress, and publish the collection, so that I can manage the collection lifecycle efficiently, ensure data quality, and control access for catalogers.
true_user_story: As a collection manager or cataloger I want to be able to see who last edited a record and when.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to create, update, and delete collections, add catalogers to collections, and monitor collection progress, so that I can efficiently manage the repository and ensure projects are executed successfully.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to restrict who has create/update/delete access to records in my collection.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to create, update, and delete collections and items, add catalogers to collections and items, and monitor collection progress, so that I can facilitate the execution of projects and ensure the repository is accurately maintained.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to restrict who has edit access to individual records in my collection.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to create and edit a collection’s DMR—defining its categories (with the authority lists that apply to each metadata field), linking a metadata profile, validating the DMR, and providing guidance to catalogers—so that the collection is correctly configured and I can monitor its progress.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to restrict access to collection metadata.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to create, update, and publish collections; add catalogers to collections and items; verify and edit metadata records; manage cataloger permissions; and monitor collection progress, so that I can efficiently oversee projects, ensure data quality, and maintain proper access control.
true_user_story: As a cataloger or collection manager I want to be able to edit an arbitrary number of Items at once so that I can make changes to all Items in a collection efficiently.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to create and update collections, verify items, perform CRUD operations on items (books, texts, media), add catalogers to collections and items, and monitor collection progress, so that I can manage the project effectively and ensure accurate metadata and content.
true_user_story: As a cataloger or collection manager I want to be able to use a grid/spreadsheet style interface so I can edit Items quickly.

user_story: As a Cataloger, I want to view incomplete items in the repository, so that I can identify and add missing structural or descriptive metadata records required by the item's assigned profile.
true_user_story: As a cataloger I want to be able to save a record without all of the required metadata so that I can start a record with the metadata I have and come back to finish it later.

user_story: As a Cataloger, I want to create, update, and delete items within a collection, verify the completeness of those items, and add catalogers to both the collection and individual items, so that I can maintain accurate metadata records and control who has editing access to each record.
true_user_story: As a user I want to be able to save a record only when the record has an identifier so that we don't end up with a bunch of empty records in the collection.

user_story: As a Cataloger, I want to create, retrieve, update, and delete items, and add catalogers to collections and items, so that I can maintain accurate metadata records and grant appropriate access to records in specific collections.
true_user_story: As a user I want to be able to publish records only when the record has a Title, Identifier, Type, and

user_story: As a Cataloger, I want to view incomplete items, verify and edit metadata records, manage cataloger permissions, create and edit collection‑level metadata, and publish collections, so that I can keep the repository’s metadata accurate, complete, and ready for users to access.
true_user_story: As a cataloger I want a list of all of the records I have access to so I can get to work quickly.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to monitor collection progress and report problems with items and collections, so that I can keep the project on schedule and address issues promptly.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to search for records based on status so I can track the progress of a collection.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to view all incomplete metadata items in a specific collection, so that I can quickly identify and prioritize records that need to be completed.
true_user_story: As a cataloger or collection manager I want to be able to search for records based on all Duke Core fields.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to create a new collection, attach a metadata profile, and assign it multiple categories (including the ‘trident’ category that grants access to all collections), so that I can organize items efficiently and enforce proper access control.
true_user_story: As an end user I want to be able to search across all collections at once.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to create, update, and delete collections through a dedicated UI, so that I can manage the repository content efficiently and keep it organized.
true_user_story: As a cataloger and a collections manager I want Trident to support Duke Core so that metadata is consistent between collections.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to use the appropriate authority lists for each metadata field, validate and edit the Digital Metadata Record (DMR) through an intuitive editing screen, view the finalized DMR, and receive on‑screen guidance throughout the process, so that I can accurately and consistently catalog items according to institutional standards.
true_user_story: As a collections manager I want required DC fields to be enforced so that all records have values for all required DC fields. ♦

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to create and manage collections and categories, assign metadata profiles and authority lists to them, add catalogers to collections and items, validate and edit DMRs, and view the DMRs with guidance, so that I can maintain consistent, accurate metadata across the system and ensure all items meet the required standards.
true_user_story: As a collections manager I want to be able to repeat repeatable DC Fields so that I can enter all of the relevant metadata. ♦

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to view incomplete items and edit their DMR through an editing screen that validates metadata against authority lists, displays the DMR to the end user, and provides guidance, so that I can complete metadata records accurately, maintain consistency across collections, and grant appropriate access by adding catalogers to collections or items.
true_user_story: As a collections manager I want Optional DC fields to be available but not required so that my collection can decide to use them or not. ♦

user_story: As a cataloger, I want a DMR editing screen that validates my input against the collection’s metadata profile and the appropriate authority lists, and provides guidance on any missing or incorrect fields, so that I can create and maintain accurate metadata records for items in the repository.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to be able to create controlled vocabularies so that they can be used as authority lists during metadata creation.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want a DMR editing screen that automatically applies the correct authority lists to each metadata field, validates the record, and displays the completed DMR to end users while providing clear guidance on any required corrections. I also want the ability to view incomplete items, create or update collections, verify items, perform full CRUD operations on items, and add catalogers to specific collections or items. This will enable me to maintain accurate, authoritative metadata records and manage cataloger access efficiently.
true_user_story: As a cataloger I want authority lists to be easily accessible and contextually relevant so it is easy to use the authority.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to edit and validate a Digital Metadata Record (DMR) through an editing screen that automatically pulls the correct authority lists for each metadata field and checks the record against the metadata profile of my assigned collection, so that I can ensure the record is accurate, consistent, and ready for display to end users while also providing clear guidance to other catalogers.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to use external authority lists so I they can be used during metadata creation.

user_story: As a Cataloger, I want to view incomplete items in the repository, so that I can identify and prioritize items that need metadata completion.
true_user_story: As a cataloger I want to be able to see/hear/watch/read the digital object I am cataloging so I don't have to move between multiple applications while I'm creating metadata.

user_story: As a scanner operator, I want to create scanning specifications and scan materials, so that I can produce high-quality digital copies that meet repository standards.
true_user_story: As a cataloger I want to be able to see/hear/watch/read all components associated on the with an item on the create/update screen so I don't have to leave the form.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to view incomplete items in a collection and monitor the collection’s progress, so that I can verify and complete metadata records efficiently and ensure the collection is ready for publication.
true_user_story: As a cataloger or collection manager I want to see all of the metadata for a record while I'm editing it so that I have the full context for the item I'm cataloging.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to monitor collection progress, verify and edit metadata records, manage cataloger permissions, edit collection‑level metadata, publish the collection, and report any problems with items or collections to the Digital Production Manager, so that the collection remains accurate, consistent, and ready for users.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want each record to have a status so I can track the progress of the records in the collection.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to monitor collection progress, so that I can track the status of each project, identify bottlenecks early, and ensure timely completion of the collection.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want statuses to include a status that flags a record as having a problem so we can track issues as the arise.

user_story: As a Content Manager, I want to add component files to Trident, automatically generate administrative metadata for each component, and create component derivatives—all through a role‑specific UI, so that I can efficiently manage content assets while ensuring that only users with the appropriate permissions can view or modify them.
true_user_story: As a cataloger I want Trident to support the UTF-8 character set so that I can use any character necessary when creating metadata.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want an editing screen that enforces the use of the correct authority lists for each metadata field, validates the DMR against the associated metadata profile, and automatically creates a structural metadata record when an item has components, so that I can confidently produce accurate, complete descriptive metadata records that comply with project standards and can be displayed to end users with clear guidance.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to map fields in inherited metadata to Duke Core fields and import a spreadsheet and create records.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want a DMR editing screen that validates my input against the appropriate authority lists, ensures each descriptive metadata record is linked to a metadata profile, enforces that any item containing components also has a structural metadata record, and provides on‑screen guidance while displaying the completed DMR to the end user, so that I can create accurate, compliant metadata records that meet the project’s standards and can be reliably consumed by users.
true_user_story: As a collection manager I want to import metadata and create records from a DCP digitazation guide.

epic: Integrate Digital Collections with other resources
user_story: As a researcher, I want to run a query that returns a ResultSet of items that are members of a collection and includes the featured item, so that I can quickly identify the key resources in that collection.
true_user_story: As a Archivist I want bi-directional linking between items in the digital collections and EAD finding aids

epic: Integrate Digital Collections with other resources
user_story: As a cataloger, I want to create a book item that contains descriptive metadata and one or more page components, and add that book to a collection (using the isMemberOfCollection relationship), so that I can organize the book within the collection and provide users with access to its individual pages.
true_user_story: As a End User I want to be able to see the physical context of an Item in a digital collection (i.e. See inside this folder; EAD)

epic: Integrate Digital Collections with other resources
user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to create, update, and publish collections, add catalogers to collections and items, verify items and metadata records, manage cataloger permissions, and monitor collection progress, so that I can efficiently oversee the project and ensure that all metadata is accurate, complete, and ready for publication.
true_user_story: As a Collection Manager I want my collection to be discoverable in the Catalog

user_story: As a Digital Production Manager, I want the system to automatically group scannable objects into components, verify each component for quality and correctness, and allow scanner operators to add components to items and generate technical metadata, so that the digitization workflow is efficient, accurate, and produces high‑quality digital assets.
true_user_story: As a Digital Production User (DPU) I want to be able to create Components.

user_story: As a metadata editor, I want to add component files to Trident and automatically generate the required administrative metadata, component derivatives, and structural metadata records—ensuring each component is linked to a metadata profile and that every item contains at least one descriptive metadata record—so that the digital collection remains accurate, compliant, and fully discoverable.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want to create Technical Metadata with for Component.

user_story: As a Digital Archivist, I want to add component files to Trident and automatically generate the required administrative metadata, component derivatives, and a Descriptive Metadata Record (DMR) that is linked to a Metadata Profile, so that every item that contains components also has a structural metadata record and at least one descriptive metadata record, ensuring the collection is complete, compliant, and easily discoverable.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want to create Preservation Metadata for a Component.

user_story: As a Digital Archivist, I want to add component files to Trident, create and manage administrative metadata and derivatives for each component, define and apply scanning specifications, scan materials, and perform full CRUD operations on items and components—including adding scanner operators to the system—so that I can efficiently ingest, catalog, preserve, and retrieve every atomic unit of digital content in a consistent, traceable, and scalable workflow.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want to upload master surrogates to a Component.

user_story: As a repository curator, I want the system to automatically prompt me to add a structural metadata record whenever I attach one or more components to an item, so that the item remains compliant with repository standards and ensures proper navigation for end users.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want to add one or more Components to an Item.

user_story: As a repository administrator, I want the system to enforce that any item containing one or more components automatically includes a structural metadata record, that each item has at least one descriptive metadata record linked to a metadata profile, and that descriptive metadata records are stored as pointer records with URI components and can be grouped in NamedSets that capture relationships with items. This will allow me to query items within a project reliably and ensure the repository’s metadata remains consistent and compliant with our standards.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want to create structural metadata for an Item so I can define the sequence of components.

user_story: As a Scanner Operator, I want to identify items and components for digitization and add them to an item, so that I can create accurate technical metadata and support quality control assessments.
true_user_story: As a Digital Production Developer (DPD) I want to group components that require digitization so that I can create "digitizable units".

user_story: As a scanner operator, I want to scan materials, add components to items, and create technical metadata while checking each component for quality and correctness, so that the collection contains accurate, well‑documented digital records that can be reliably retrieved, updated, or deleted by the system.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want to check-out/check-in groups of Components.

user_story: As a scanner operator, I want to digitize materials, add components to an item, and automatically create technical metadata while checking components for quality and correctness, so that the items are accurately represented and validated against the system's validation rules.
true_user_story: As a DPD I want to verify the correctness and completeness of components so that I can ensure quality standards are met.

user_story: As a Digital Production Manager, I want a unified interface that lets me identify items and components, create and manage scanning specifications, add and assign scanner operators, and perform quality‑control assessments, so that I can efficiently oversee the entire digitization workflow and ensure every scan meets our quality standards.
true_user_story: As a DPD I want to capture digitization statistics so that I can monitor Operator efficiency and make projections for future projects.

user_story: As a Trident Administrator, I want to add component files, create administrative metadata, and generate component derivatives through the Trident UI, so that I can manage the component lifecycle and ensure that other user roles have the appropriate permissions to perform their tasks.
true_user_story: As a DPU I want Trident to create derivatives for me when I upload a Master.

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to monitor collection progress, verify and edit metadata records, manage cataloger permissions, edit collection‑level metadata, publish the collection, and report problems with items and collections to the Digital Production Manager, so that I can ensure the collection is accurate, complete, and ready for publication while keeping the workflow running smoothly.
true_user_story: As a User I want to monitor the progress of a digital collection project.

user_story: As a Digital Production Manager, I want to manage the digitization workflow by identifying items and components, grouping scannable objects, creating scanning specifications, assigning scanner operators, and performing quality‑control assessments, so that I can efficiently produce high‑quality digital assets for the collection.
true_user_story: As a user I want to see how many Items have been digitized.♦

user_story: As a Digital Production Manager, I want to create, retrieve, update, and delete items and components, add scanner operators to the system, and perform quality‑control assessments of scans, so that I can efficiently run the digitization process and ensure accurate, high‑quality results.
true_user_story: As a user I want to see how many digitized Items have been described.♦

user_story: As a Digital Production Manager, I want to view incomplete items, manage item records (create, retrieve, update, delete), verify and edit metadata, control cataloger permissions, edit collection‑level metadata, publish the collection, monitor collection progress, and report problems with items and collections, so that I can ensure the collection is accurate, complete, and ready for publication efficiently.
true_user_story: As a user I want to see how many Items have been published.♦

user_story: As a Collection Manager, I want to view a dashboard that displays the progress of each collection and lists all items that are members of that collection, so that I can monitor project execution and ensure every item is correctly associated with its collection.
true_user_story: As Collection Manager or Sponsor I want to describe the collection.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to use predefined authority lists for metadata fields, validate and edit DMRs through an editing screen, view DMRs, receive guidance on cataloging, scan materials, create/update items and components, add scanner operators, and define relationships such as isMemberOfCollection, hasCollectionMember, and isFeaturedItem, so that I can maintain consistent, accurate, and well‑structured metadata and item relationships in the repository.
true_user_story: As a Preservation Librarian I want the relationship between masters and derivatives to be managed by the system so I don't have to.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want to assign a metadata profile to a collection and its items, link categories to descriptive metadata records, and ensure each image item contains descriptive metadata and one or more image components, so that the collection is consistently organized, members belong to at most one collection, and all items meet the required metadata standards.
true_user_story: As a Metadata Librarian I want to define the metadata schema for a collection.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want a DMR editing screen that validates each field against the appropriate authority list, applies the collection‑specific metadata profile (including required fields and categories), and displays the completed DMR to the end user while providing guidance on any missing or incorrect data, so that I can ensure every item is correctly catalogued, belongs to at most one collection, and meets all required metadata standards.
true_user_story: As a Metadata librarian I want to define Authorities to be used for specific fields in a collection MD Schema.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want an interactive DMR editing screen that automatically evaluates each metadata field against defined validation rules and authority lists, displays the resulting DMR to me, and provides contextual guidance, so that I can quickly identify and correct invalid or inconsistent metadata, ensuring high‑quality, consistent catalog records.
true_user_story: As a Metadata Librarian I want to define validation rules for specific fields in a collection.

user_story: As a cataloger, I want the DMR editing screen to automatically evaluate my descriptive metadata against defined validation rules and authority lists, and display the results with clear guidance, so that I can correct errors and ensure consistency before finalizing the record.
true_user_story: As a Metadata Librarian I want validation rules enforced when MD is submitted.
